Justification: Resilience was defined as return rate to equilibrium governed by the leading eigenvalue of the community matrix with resilience = -max(λB) and described as return time and return rate following displacement from equilibrium. Resistance was quantified as a determinant based index of overall variability in community interactions using det(V∞-Σ) divided by det(V∞). The data came from a stationary fish trap anchored to the seabed 200 m from the Camogli coast of the Ligurian Sea which is a shallow nearshore marine environment over the continental shelf.

Resistance was quantified as Rs = NDVI 2011 divided by NDVI 2010 and recovery was quantified as Rc = NDVI 2012 or 2013 or 2014 divided by NDVI 2011 with higher Rc values indicating post drought rebound relative to 2011. The study area riparian systems include floodplains wetlands and bottomland hardwood forests and the riparian vegetation sampling used an EPA riparian zone mask within this basin.

Framework: ecosystem health = vigor + organization + resilience + stress
Justification: Resilience is not quantified from recovery dynamics but is computed as an ecosystem resilience index using land use proportions and fixed land use type coefficients with ERI = sum Ai × RCi and RCi taken from Table 2 so resilience is inferred. Habitat is multi because the resilience related inputs and coefficients explicitly span multiple land use types including cropland forestland grassland water construction land and bare land and the analysis is conducted across all counties in Guizhou Province.

Resilience was calculated as the network resilience parameter βeff which the study describes as a one-dimensional mathematical model quantifiable from an ecological network and it defines the state of an ecosystem against disturbances such as loss in interactions species interaction weight or combinations therefrom. The study area included savannah and shrubland at lower elevations and an intermix of indigenous bushland pasture human settlement subsistence farming and large scale agricultural plantations at higher elevations so no single IUCN habitat type dominates.

Resilience was quantified as a resilience index defined as the change in relative vegetation cover during the year after trampling expressed as a fraction of the change in cover that occurred 2 weeks after trampling. This uses measured cover before trampling and at 2 weeks and 1 year after trampling. The study used 18 vegetation types in five separate mountain regions in the United States and Table 1 includes both overstorey canopy cover and understorey vegetation cover indicating multiple habitat contexts rather than a single dominant habitat type.

Resilience is quantified as ER = sum of Ai × RCi where RCi is derived by combining expert scores and previous studies so resilience is inferred from land use type areas rather than measured recovery dynamics. The study area is the Jiaozhou Bay coastal zone defined as townships within 10 km of the coastline in Qingdao Shandong Province so the dominant habitat type is Coastal.

Resilience is defined as a function of resistance and recovery with resistance described as capacity to withstand disturbance and recovery described as capacity to return to a pre disturbance state. Resistance is quantified as disturbance impact using the log response ratio of pre to post disturbance coral cover and recovery is quantified as annual recovery rate from linear models fit to coral cover in the 5 year period following disturbance and as recovery completeness when the final data point is at least 90% of pre disturbance cover. The data are from tropical coral reefs globally and the studies are assigned to marine ecoregions for coastal and shelf waters which corresponds to Marine.Neritic.

Resilience was quantified as the maximum autotrophic biomass growth rate rmax described as a proxy for the resilience of primary producers and used to calculate daily maximum percent increase in biomass and biomass doubling time. This corresponds to recovery rate because rmax is an intrinsic growth rate governing post disturbance regrowth. The data are from rivers and streams with flood disturbances and river bed sediment mobilization thresholds indicating an inland freshwater system which matches Wetland. The study subjects are autotrophic biomass described as algae and periphyton which are treated here as Chromista.

Resilience was quantified as post disturbance cell accrual using least squared rates of change in natural log transformed cell densities during each post spate recovery period and as convergence of disturbed communities with a temporally changing undisturbed control community within 3 to 9 d. Resistance was measured as the change in algal biomass and taxonomic structure across a disturbance including cell density reduction and displacement of taxonomic structure. The study site draws water from Harts Run draining a basin of mixed hardwood forest and the experimental stream facility is located in Bernheim Forest Nature Preserve.

Resilience was not quantified from recovery dynamics and was instead inferred because the introduction states that functional evenness has been used as a proxy for the resilience of communities and the discussion links lower diversity in invaded communities to lower resilience to disturbance. Habitat type is Multi because the study area is described as a landscape characterised by agriculture forest shrubland urban and wetland areas and sampling was stratified across agricultural forest and urban land classes.

Target variable type: Land use; Abiotic parameter; Ecosystem function; Physiological indicator; Regional network parameter; Socio-eco parameter
Justification: Resilience is quantified as an index ERI computed from the disturbance carrying recovery dimensions using ERI = ωdi EDF + ωci ECC + ωri ERC with EDF derived from disturbance indicators and ECC and ERC from carrying and recovery indicators so resilience is inferred from proxies rather than measured as recovery after a defined disturbance. The study area is the Dongting Lake region described as river and lake floodplains centered around Dongting Lake and the analysis includes Dongting Lake waters and water land transition zones so the dominant habitat for the resilience assessment is wetland.

Resilience is quantified from the ARx coefficient alpha where a larger absolute value indicates a slow rate of returning to an equilibrium state from a disturbance and thus low resilience which is a recovery rate concept. The study uses NDVI anomaly time series from 2001 to 2022 as the ecosystem status variable in the ARx model. The study area is described as one of the driest regions in the world with sparse vegetation and desertification and vegetation in plains and basins is mostly dry and saline herbaceous vegetation and semi shrubs which supports coding the dominant habitat as Desert.

Resilience was defined as return time because the study states it considers engineering resilience as the return time of individual plant species to the equilibrium after abrupt change events and it determined return time as the number of years for which control and treatment runs differed significantly after the change event. Resistance was also treated as part of the response because the introduction states the PBM simulates response to salt and water stress as resistance and the SDM can underestimate persistence in temporarily unsuitable habitat conditions as resistance. The habitat is Coastal because the study investigated coastal vegetation at the German Baltic Sea and the study site is a low lying peninsula protected by a sea wall with a coastal reed belt and salt marsh conditions.

Quantification is recovery rate because the study states resilience as rate of recovery and analyzes recovery rates as the slope of perennial algal cover over time and reports that herbivores and algal richness increased recovery rates. Quantification also includes recovery degree because the study reports effects on the rate and degree of recovery and compares cover and dry mass at 18 months and 27 months relative to ambient cover in control plots. Habitat type is Coastal because the study is in a rocky intertidal zone and describes a rocky intertidal community at Bodega Marine Reserve.

Quantification is based on demographic recovery because the damping ratio is described as describing the rate at which a population perturbed from its stationary equilibrium converges back to its asymptotic growth trajectory and the transient envelope quantifies the magnitude by which the short term dynamics of a population deviate from its long term trajectory. Habitat type is Marine.Neritic because the study surveys coral assemblages in reef communities and coral assemblages in southern Japan and eastern Australia including Okinawa Heron Island Kochi and the Solitary Islands Marine Park.

Resilience was approximated by modeling and comparing the current and future distribution of suitable habitats expressed as the number of climatically suitable cells for each lineage. This is an inferred quantification because it is based on SDM projections of climatic suitability rather than observed recovery dynamics. Habitat type is wetland because the habitat of Primula farinosa encompasses calcareous fens and edges of streams or lakes.

Framework: resilience = capacity to remain within a stability domain or state
Justification: Resilience was defined as the size of the region between the desired stable state and the separatrix and in the model it was operationalized as the difference between the unstable chlorophyll equilibrium and the lower clear water equilibrium and as the P input rate at which the system flips between clear and turbid states. This corresponds to a threshold magnitude of phosphorus input that can be withstood without shifting to the alternative high chlorophyll state. The study system is northern temperate lakes in the Northern Highlands Lake District of Wisconsin and related experimental lakes and these are freshwater inland waters which match the Wetland habitat category.

Framework: resilience = capacity to absorb environmental changes without altering its main functions
Justification: Potential resilience was not measured as recovery after disturbance but was estimated by combining species richness and functional redundancy of functional traits and then discretely classifying these values into terciles. High environmental quality sites were within cities and were defined using urban green cover and heterogeneity light pollution and potential resilience of avian communities with point counts positioned in a gradient of urbanized areas.

Quantification is inferred because the study proposes the Persistence Index to assess capacity to maintain functions after disturbances and calculates it from response trait richness and response trait abundance and redundancy of response traits rather than measuring recovery dynamics. Habitat type is forest because the empirical application calculates the index for forest stands using the Third Spanish National Forest Inventory across all forests in Peninsular Spain and the Balearic Islands.

Framework: resilience = distance to the boundary of the attraction basin
Justification: Quantification is latitude because the paper states that it mathematically defines resilience as the distance to the boundary of the attraction basin and repeatedly operationalizes this as distance to tipping points. Habitat type is NA because the resilience analysis uses mutualistic network topology and proportional abundance data from eight locations worldwide without specifying a single dominant IUCN habitat type for the data used to quantify resilience.

Resilience is evaluated through sea level rise thresholds derived from landscape metrics and principal component analysis of those metrics rather than from observed recovery dynamics. The study area is described as the Grand Bay National Estuarine Research Reserve with extensive salt marshes and adjacent estuarine open water which is a coastal wetland habitat.

Resilience was derived from an autoregressive modeling framework where δ captures temporal dependency and a larger δ implies slower recovery and therefore lower resilience and the resilience index was expressed as 1 – δ. This operationalization uses recovery dynamics in NDVI time series rather than a static proxy. The study area vegetation types include forests shrublands savannas grasslands wetlands and croplands indicating multiple habitat types rather than a single dominant habitat.

Framework: ecosystem health = vigor + organization + resilience + services
Target variable type: Ecosystem function; Regional network parameter; Land use
Justification: Resilience is not measured as recovery dynamics but as a coefficient derived from land use classes and NDVI using RCi = NDVIi/NDVI-mean j × RCj with fixed resilience coefficients for land classes so the measurement is inferred. Habitat is agricultural because the study focuses on tea production and tea plantations in Fujian Province and discusses eco tea gardens and tea plantation area and tea yield as core outcomes.

Resilience was assessed after a simulated disturbance where all above ground cordgrass biomass was clipped and all mussels were removed and the study then monitored cordgrass survival and regrowth 10 days and 13 months following the simulated disturbance. This quantifies resistance via survival shortly after disturbance and recovery degree via regrowth measured as the number of shoots after disturbance. The study site is a US saltmarsh on Sapelo Island that is regularly flooded through tidal inundation which corresponds to a coastal habitat.

Framework: community stability = sensitivity + asymmetry + resistance + resilience
Justification: Resilience was defined as the level of productivity relative to its undisturbed state after an ECE and was calculated from Yn Ye and Ye+1 using Equation 3 which compares post ECE ANPP to the non ECE baseline. The study focused on natural grassland and compiled 36 long term observational ANPP datasets from alpine grassland on the Tibetan Plateau the Eurasian steppe and the North American prairie.

Table 1 states that the diagonal entries represent the percentage of occasions where a sample did not undergo transition and indicate resilience which quantifies resilience as the ability to remain unchanged in community membership. The study area is explicitly the Sonoran Desert in Arizona USA and the data are from the Santa Rita Experimental Range within that desert landscape.

Ecological resilience is described as the amount of rainfall change a regular patterned state can withstand before it flips which is quantified as a minus ac,N which matches a threshold to another stability region. Engineering resilience is described as the speed at which vegetation patches restore their shape after disturbance and is linked to critical slowing down which matches recovery rate. The model is motivated by dryland semi arid ecosystems and focuses on desertification into a desert state without vegetation. The study uses a reaction diffusion dryland vegetation model with rainfall driven resource scarcity and vegetation patches separated by bare soil.

Framework: UEH = pressure + vigor + organization + resilience + service
Justification: Resilience is not measured as recovery dynamics but is an indicator within the P-VOR-S indicator system where resilience is represented by habitat quality and UEHI is computed by aggregating normalized indicators with weights. The study states that resilience is expressed in terms of habitat quality and UEHI is calculated from pressure vigor organization resilience and service indicators so resilience quantification is inferred from proxies rather than observed recovery. The study focuses on urban ecosystem health in coastal cities and repeatedly frames the system as urban ecosystems with land use classification and urban centers used for validation so the dominant habitat type is urban.

Quantification is based on the statement that resilience was defined as the rate and degree of native community recovery from disturbance and they measured the degree to which native cover and richness in disturbed plots matched adjacent undisturbed plots in each post treatment year. Habitat type is grassland because experiments were conducted at grassland sites in natural bunchgrass communities and the methods describe all ranges as bunchgrass systems.

The paper states that Holling called resilience the height of the basin of attraction and it describes this as the height of V0 in the ball in cup diagram. It also states that latitude is the width of the basin of attraction and it presents three metrics of stability including basin width and basin height. Habitat type is not specified because the work uses three classic ecological models and simulations without field sampling locations or habitat descriptions.

Framework: ecological adaptability cycle = potential + resilience + connectivity
Justification: Resilience was not quantified as recovery after a defined disturbance but was inferred because the study states that resilience patches were identified as those with significant long term NDVI increase and that continuous green trend indicates stronger resilience. Habitat type is Grassland because the study area description states grassland is the major land use type and the results state the primary ecological sources were mainly concentrated in the middle and the land use type was mainly grassland.

Framework: resilience = ability to avoid qualitative state changes
Justification: Resilience was defined as the ability to avoid qualitative state changes and was evaluated using extirpation thresholds and fire rotation demarcating regeneration failure or success which indicates a threshold beyond which the original community cannot be maintained. The case study region was the subalpine forests of Yellowstone National Park and the model was parameterized for this forest system.

Framework: ecosystem health = vigour + organisation + resilience + services
Justification: Resilience was not quantified from recovery dynamics and was instead assigned using land use categories because the methods state that the vegetation recovery coefficient and resistance index were determined based on relevant studies and values associated with the respective urban expansion areas and Table 2 reports resilience values by land use. The habitat context is dominated by urban expansion and impervious surfaces because the study evaluates ecological security patterns based on urban expansion in Chengdu and classifies land use including impermeable surfaces urban and other constructed areas.

Resilience was defined as combining resistance and recovery and the study calculated resistance as the ratio of regrowth biomass during drought to pre-drought biomass and calculated recovery and resilience as ratios of biomass after drought to biomass before drought. These ratios quantify resistance and recovery degree using above-ground biomass harvested in the plots. The site was described as a species-rich pasture and semi-natural grasslands in the Swiss Jura Mountains which corresponds to grassland habitat.

Resistance was quantified as RWDr divided by RWpreDr and recovery was quantified as RWpostDr divided by RWDr and resilience was quantified as RWpostDr divided by RWpreDr and recovery period was quantified as Yearrecover minus YearDr with RW defined as tree ring width. The sampling sites were described as three secondary coniferous broad leaved mixed forests and tree ring cores were collected from mature trees in these forests.

Resilience was evaluated by comparing the grass layer before the first fire in 2015 and after seven annual mid-dry-season fires in 2022 and the study states that after seven annual burns community composition remained unchanged and grass density doubled evidencing the grass layer's resilience to repeated fires. This corresponds to resistance because the system maintained its composition under repeated fire. The habitat is grassland because the study selected grassland plots in grassland patches with grass-dominated vegetation and continuous ground layer locally classified as campo sujo.

Framework: engineering resilience = time of complete recovery
Justification: The study states that engineering resilience is defined as the time of complete recovery tc and tc is predicted by curve fitting of forest recovery trajectories to an exponential decay function. The recovery trajectories are based on time series Landsat NBR and the stable state is assumed as the level of pre disturbed or undisturbed reference area. The study area is described as pine plantation forest with overstories dominated by Pinus taiwanensis and other tree species.

Quantification is based on the stated definition that resilience is the capacity to resist and recover from stress and the methods that calculate a resilience index as the ratio between plant response under drought versus ambient treatment with significant differences to 1 described as full recovery. Habitat type is supported by the site description stating the study was conducted in a native grass-shrub rangeland.

Quantification: Resistance; Recovery degree; Recovery time; Recovery rate; Other
Justification: Resilience indices were calculated from standardized ring width series for the 2005 and 2012 droughts including resistance defined as capacity to sustain growth during the stress episode and recovery defined as ability to recover after disturbance and resilience defined as ability to reach pre disturbance growth levels plus recovery period and average recovery rate and total growth reduction. The study took place in two Mediterranean shrublands with sparse trees in semi arid north eastern Spain.

Resilience is quantified as a composite index value Ci from an improved TOPSIS model using an indicator system rather than system recovery dynamics. The paper states that the framework encompasses four key domains resistance recovery adaptation and renewal and Table 1 lists socio ecological indicators such as per capita water resources PM2.5 and tourism income used to compute TSESR. The study area is described as an arid region with vast deserts including the Taklamakan Desert and the assessment is for Xinjiang arid areas.

Resilience was quantified using early warning indicators linked to critical slowing down including autocorrelation at lag-1 and standard deviation and density ratio calculated as trends on residual time series which the text describes as increased recovery time after a minor perturbation. The virtual system is a shallow lake simulated with PCLake and the study focuses on lake water quality variables such as chlorophyll-a transparency Secchi depth oxygen and nutrient stocks which correspond to an inland freshwater habitat.

Resistance was quantified using pairwise comparisons of community dissimilarity between harvesting treatments and uncut control stands over time which the authors state they interpreted as a measure of resistance and potential resilience. Recovery was quantified as similarity of postharvest beetle assemblage composition to uncut control stands including statements that higher retention had similar compositions to uncut stands within five years and that five-year recovery differed among cover types. The habitat is boreal mixedwood forests at the EMEND research site described as forest cover classes with deciduous and conifer canopies in northwest Alberta.

Resilience was quantified from temporal community data using annual versus decadal turnover where the temporal beta Simpson annual versus decadal turnover gives a measure for resilience and from recovery to initial conditions where the functional group distribution returned to the same as at the beginning. The study also quantified resistance as stability of composition and structure and used a between-plot resilience metric as the product of annual replacement and overall similarity. The habitat is calcareous grassland described as relic calcareous grasslands and a uniformly managed grassland complex near Maastricht at the Belgian-Dutch border.

Resilience was quantified as return time because the study states that resilience was assessed by return time and it calculated return time as 1 divided by the subdominant eigenvalue. The habitat is coastal because the study site is a rocky intertidal community in the middle intertidal zone of Tatoosh Island.

Resistance was estimated as |Ye−Yn|/|Yn| and recovery was estimated as |Ye−Yn|/|Ye+1−Yn| using annual above ground net primary productivity in precipitation event years and the year after. Long term invariability was estimated as mean ANPP divided by the standard deviation of ANPP. The study plots are described as six 1 ha grasslands at the Kellogg Biological Station Long Term Ecological Research site and the study evaluates productivity resilience in this temperate grassland community.

Framework: resilience = capacity to tolerate fire without shifting to an alternative ecosystem state
Justification: Resilience was assessed using post-fire tree seedling establishment described as a key indicator of forest resilience rather than by measuring recovery dynamics of a state variable through time. The study defines post-fire resilience as the capacity to tolerate fire without shifting to an alternative ecosystem state and then evaluates it via establishment rates across plots. The study area is described as subalpine forests and mountainous conifer forest dominated by Engelmann spruce subalpine fir and lodgepole pine.

Resilience was quantified using grass greenness with standardized impact and absolute recovery rates as proxies for ecological resilience to drought with impact defined as the change in grass greenness percent between wet season 2018 and dry season 2019 and recovery rates defined as the absolute change in grass greenness per day after each dry season. The habitat was cattle pastures described as actively used cattle pastures planted with C4 exotic grasses and established by logging and burning dry forests.

The study defines resilience as the rate a variable returns to its reference condition after a perturbation and it quantifies this using post fire return toward pre fire demographic rates and compositional recovery. It also evaluates resilience sensu Holling as whether altered states form and persist under changing disturbance regimes which corresponds to assessing tipping into alternate vegetation states. The data come from monodominant pine forest permanent plots in the Cordillera Central described as tropical montane pine forest.

Resilience was quantified from simulated post hurricane coral cover because the methods state that the net outcome of hurricane induced disturbance and recovery on total coral cover was evaluated after a 50 year simulation. The study also quantified a threshold based resilience mechanism because it states that a small increase in grazing shifted the reef beyond a bifurcation point and that mangroves shifted the threshold level of mortality upwards by 2–4%. The habitat is Marine.Neritic because the model and field data are from coral reefs and forereefs at depths 3–6 m and 7–15 m and include sites such as Glovers Atoll Belize and reefs in Bonaire and the Bahamas.

Quantification is resistance because the study states it aimed to project resilience and it reports survival probabilities and mortality during constant and dynamic heatwave exposure rather than time to return to a prior state. Habitat type is Marine.Neritic because the study system is subtidal Western Baltic Sea and Kiel Fjord shallow water habitats and the experiments used seawater from the fjord and regional shallow water communities.

Target variable type: Ecosystem function; Abiotic parameter; Land use; Socio-eco parameter
Justification: Quantification is inferred because the study constructed an indicator system and calculated FER as a composite of HD ND GD RF RFS RFM RS using weighted normalized level-2 indicators rather than measuring recovery after a defined disturbance. Habitat type is Agricultural because the study focus and data base map are farmland ecosystems and the farmland distribution was derived from the MODIS Land Cover product.

Target variable type: Regional network parameter; Ecosystem function; Land use
Justification: Resilience was not quantified from observed recovery dynamics of a state variable over time but was evaluated using proxy indicators including robustness and connectivity of an ecological network and ecosystem services values before and after simulated urban regeneration. The study states that it uses robustness to investigate stability or how resilient the ecosystem is to disturbances and reports changes in robustness curves and connectivity indices rather than recovery time or rate of an ecological state variable. The study area is described as the Nanning City District with old urban buildings dominating in the north central region and construction land covering 44.29 percent indicating an urban habitat context for the resilience related analysis.

Resilience was defined as recovery of pre-disturbance light levels and bare soil and was quantified as a statistical comparison of pre vs post disturbance levels which corresponds to recovery degree. The study site is described as an oak Quercus garryana savanna remnant in the Cowichan Garry Oak reserve on southeastern Vancouver Island which matches the Savanna habitat type.

Resilience was quantified as recovery of ecosystem productivity because the study reports that aboveground net primary productivity recovered fully and returned to predrought levels in a single year and it calculated response ratios comparing ANPP in 2017 with ANPP averaged across 2006 to 2014. The habitat is a savanna because the site vegetation is characterized as Knobthorn Marula savanna and the study focuses on the herbaceous component of this savanna grassland in Kruger National Park.

Resilience is quantified by an entropy weighted evaluation index system that comprises resistance adaptability and recovery and uses 13 indicators including population density PM2.5 industrial emissions waste treatment public transport vehicles and green space rather than measuring recovery dynamics of an ecosystem state variable. The study area is the Yellow River Basin which the introduction describes as including plateau desert and wetland so the habitat source is multiple habitat types rather than a single dominant one.

Quantification is based on the study definition that resistance is the capacity to survive disturbance through persistence and resilience is the capacity to recover to pre-disturbance abundance levels through recruitment and the response variable is the pre to post fire response ratio of density change. Habitat type is shrubland because the study setting is described as biodiverse Mediterranean type shrublands kwongan of the Geraldton Sandplain region of southwestern Australia.

Resilience was measured by subtracting control canonical scores from treatment canonical scores and plotting the deviation from baseline through time which operationalizes return toward control or baseline assemblage values as the degree of recovery. The study sites were rocky reefs at 6 to 12 m depth including broken reef and Ecklonia radiata forest which are shallow nearshore marine habitats on the continental shelf.

Resilience was defined as rate of recovery after disturbance and in stable plots it was determined as recovery rate using a quadratic regression estimating percent recovery month to 50% of controls. The study site was a microtidal salt marsh dominated by Spartina alterniflora and the paper repeatedly describes it as a salt marsh which is a wetland habitat.

Resilience was evaluated as recovery of ecosystem function because the study states that resilience is the capacity for recovery of function and reports complete recovery in ecosystem function total ANPP one year post drought. This corresponds to recovery degree because total ANPP returned to the control level in the recovery year. The habitat is grassland because the study site is described as a native tallgrass prairie and a central U.S. grassland at Konza Prairie Biological Station.

Target variable type: Ecosystem function; Biomass; Demography parameter; Growth
Justification: Resilience was quantified from direct recovery dynamics because delayed feeding treatments showed recovery measured by increases in respiration and protein accretion and body lengths and the text reports recovery rates within 0.9 fold of respiration rate and 0.8 fold of protein accretion rate of control larvae. Resistance was also quantified because unfed larvae were still capable of sustaining respiration and conserving whole body lipid and protein content during food deprivation. The habitat is Marine.Neritic because larvae were reared in seawater at the Wrigley Marine Science Center on Santa Catalina Island and bacteria were collected from Pacific Ocean waters off Santa Catalina Island.

Framework: resilience = ability to return to a predisturbance state
Justification: Resilience was defined as the ability of a community to return to a predisturbance state and was tested by comparing total cell numbers aggregates flagellates and alpha and beta diversity between day 20 and day 27. This corresponds to recovery degree because it evaluates whether the state at day 27 returned to values similar to those before the pulse disturbance. The source community and experimental medium were lake water from Lake Maggiore which is a freshwater lake habitat fitting the Wetland category.

Resilience was quantified as community similarity using 1 minus Bray Curtis dissimilarity with resilience defined as before to after similarity and resistance defined as before to during similarity and the Methods state resilience can be partitioned into resistance and recovery and that resilience is used as synonymous with recovery. The habitat is described as low elevation rocky intertidal benches on the coasts of Oregon and northern California which corresponds to a coastal habitat type.

Quantification is recovery degree because the study reports that leaf area and leaf area ratio recovered within a year to near pre-treatment levels and that root starch concentrations had returned to pre-treatment values. Habitat type is Other because the saplings were transplanted to an open field at the University of Alberta experimental station into excavated plots refilled with a sand-peat mixture which does not match the provided natural habitat categories.

Target variable type: Ecosystem function; Abiotic parameter; Land use; Socio-eco parameter
Justification: Resilience was not quantified from recovery dynamics but was a component of an ecological vulnerability assessment using the SRP model with indicators including FVC and NPP and a biological abundance index. The study area includes multiple land use and cover types because the EAI equation explicitly uses forestland grassland water body cropland urban land and bare land so the dominant habitat type cannot be reduced to a single IUCN category.

Quantification is based on basin of attraction depth and breadth because the introduction states that depth and breadth of the basin are metaphors for resilience and the methods state that geometric properties such as size of the valley depth and slope define the properties of resilience and they map tipping points and bistable regions across tetracycline concentrations. Recovery rate is explicitly calculated as days required to reach equilibrium and invariability is measured as variation in E. coli frequency over time. Habitat type is Other because the work is a laboratory microbial co-culture in defined liquid growth medium and no IUCN natural habitat type is described.

Resilience was measured using the largest connectivity subgraph score S after sequential node removals where nodes become inactive and are removed after a single attack and all connected edges are also removed which quantifies the network ability to resist interference. The study also states that S and network efficiency E were normalized and combined to calculate the degree of resilience R which does not match a single recovery metric. The land use map and data classify the study area into woodland cultivated land water grassland construction land and unused land indicating multiple habitat types rather than one dominant IUCN habitat category.

Quantification is based on resistance and recovery time because the study compares whether disturbances trigger a shift versus return and it reports that structure removing disturbances rapidly regained predisturbance levels while structure retaining disturbances triggered persistent shifts and it also quantified the time taken to reach equilibrium. Habitat type is Marine.Neritic because the study focuses on coral reef ecosystems and models benthic cover on hard substrate on coral reefs.

Resilience was quantified as proportional change following release of the disturbance with Resilience = SRT3/SRT2 which is a recovery degree measure. Resistance was quantified as proportional change during disturbance with Resistance = SRT2/SRT1. Stability was quantified as constancy over the disturbance duration using the coefficient of variation over the three time periods which is an invariability type measure. The study landscapes were in an agricultural region where native vegetation has been cleared for farming pastoral cropping and horticulture.

Resilience was defined as the rate at which a system recovers following disturbance and was quantified using the exponential rate of increase r from a first order model and by time of recovery to pre disturbance levels using Dunnett's test. The habitat source was laboratory streams supplied with water from a spring fed pond and inoculated with rocks collected from Walker Branch a stream on the Oak Ridge reservation so the closest IUCN category among the provided options is Other.

Resilience was quantified as functional resilience defined as the recovery rate of total biomass using measurements on days 6 and 21 and as compositional resilience defined as compositional recovery using Bray Curtis similarity between day 6 and day 21. The habitat is a freshwater plankton food web representative of Dutch ditches which are freshwater aquatic systems consistent with Wetland.

Resilience was quantified as recovery after repeated biomass removals because the study states it quantified functional space occupancy and biomass and compositional recovery after pulse disturbances and reports planned contrasts of disturbance effects on open space and biomass and PERMANOVA treatment effects on composition. This includes recovery degree because it compares disturbed treatments to undisturbed controls after a 1 month recovery period and recovery rate because it evaluates speed of functional recovery across latitude and across early and late assembly timescales. The habitat is Marine.Neritic because the study system is subtidal sessile marine invertebrates on nearshore Pacific coasts and panels were suspended at 1 m depth below floating docks in marinas.

Resilience was quantified as an indicator within a vulnerability model using NDVI slope because the methods state that the resilience layer is evaluated using the standardized slope of annual maximum NDVI from 1990 to 2020. This is an inferred quantification because the study uses NDVI slope to represent resilience rather than measuring recovery after a defined disturbance. The study area is described as an arid oasis threatened by the Kumtag Desert and including a transition zone with the Gobi desert and the dominant context is arid desert surroundings. Therefore the dominant habitat type for the resilience related vegetation index data is coded as Desert.

The study states a simple definition of resilience as recovery of a response variable to pre fire levels within a time period shorter than the average fire return interval and it reports that biogeochemical proxies and pollen assemblages recovered to pre fire values within c. 50 to 80 years. The study area is described as a subalpine forest watershed and subalpine forests in the Silver Lake watershed are dominated by coniferous tree taxa.

Resilience is defined as the ability of a population to return to its previous condition following disturbance and the study evaluates population response to annual poison baiting by simulating biomass trajectories and whether biomass reduction targets are met by specified years. The quantification therefore uses the time dependent response of biomass under sustained annual control which aligns with recovery rate rather than a static proxy. The case study system is Utah Lake described as a large open shallow lake and the data and model are based on carp control efforts in that lake. A shallow lake is classified as Wetland in the IUCN habitat scheme.

Return rate was quantified as the inverse of return time where return time was the number of days total chlorophyll a observations were outside the 90% highest posterior density interval predicted from the reference period. Sensitivity was quantified as the maximum difference between observed and predicted total chlorophyll a which the paper defines as the magnitude of response to perturbation and as the inverse of resistance. The study sites are described as small fishless ponds that are dystrophic surrounded by extensive mats of Sphagnum and stratified in summer with anoxic hypolimnia which matches a wetland pond habitat.
